name: Patient B
presenting_complaint: Sudden severe headache that started 90 minutes ago while gardening; describes it as 'the worst headache of my life' but is now sitting up and conversational.
history: Hypertension on losartan, otherwise well. Took paracetamol at home and the pain has eased a little. NRS 3/10 currently. Alert (AVPU=A). No prior migraines. Mild neck stiffness when asked, otherwise denies focal weakness, vomiting, or visual change. Daughter on the phone insists 'mum never complains, this is unusual.'

trajectory.state: Alert and conversational. Reports headache 3/10 since paracetamol. BP 158/92, HR 80. Mild photophobia if pressed.
Headache returning to 6/10 as paracetamol wears off. BP creeping to 168/96. Now reports brief vomit in the bathroom; did not flag it.
Drowsy when re-examined, slow to answer. GCS now 13 (E3 V4 M6). BP 175/100, HR 64. Neck stiffness pronounced.
Acute deterioration: vomits, becomes confused (AVPU=V), pupils now mildly anisocoric. Risk of rebleed. Needs urgent CT and neurosurgery referral.

red_herrings: Initial NRS only 3/10 after paracetamol — easy to under-triage as tension headache
Stoic persona masks the original 'thunderclap' severity
